paper_title: 3D label-free imaging and analysis of Pinus pollen grains using optical diffraction tomography

methods.description: Process for collecting, drying, preserving, and preparing pine pollen grains for optical diffraction tomography imaging.

steps.description: The pollen grains were collected during late April from blooming pine trees of a local farm.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pollen grains from pine trees

steps.description: Pollen grains were placed in containers by gently shaking them off the male cones of the trees.
They were dried for approximately 2 hours at room temperature
then sealed and preserved in a dark cabinet at room temperature.
In order to prepare pollen grains for imaging, they were suspended in a drop of RI-matching oil (Series A, Cargille Labs, United States)
steps.searchable_summary: Suspend pollen in RI-matching oil for imaging

steps.description: and sandwiched between two cover glasses (C024501, Matsunami Glass Ind. Ltd., Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: Create sample slide by sandwiching suspension between cover glasses

steps.description: Imaging was conducted using pollen grains that were not aggregated with other particles.
steps.searchable_summary: Select non-aggregated pollen grains on the slide for imaging

methods.method_name: 3D Refractive Index Map Acquisition and Reconstruction using ODT
methods.description: Process using Optical Diffraction Tomography (ODT) to acquire holographic images of pollen samples illuminated from different angles and reconstruct 3D refractive index maps.

steps.description: Using ODT, the 3D RI map of a sample was reconstructed using holographic images of a sample that had been illuminated from different angles (see Fig. 1a for the schematic diagram). The acquisition of phase and intensity images was performed using an off-axis Mach-Zehnder interferometric setup with a digital micromirror device (DMD) installed (HT-1S, Tomocube Inc., Republic of Korea) (see Fig. 1b).
steps.searchable_summary: Set up off-axis Mach-Zehnder interferometric system with DMD for ODT
inputs: ODT system components (Mach-Zehnder interferometer, DMD, objective lens, tube lens, camera, reference beam source)

steps.description: The incident angles of illumination were rapidly scanned by projecting a series of binary holograms on the installed DMD .
The light scattered by the sample was transmitted to the camera along an imaging system consisting of an objective lens (60, numerical aperture = 0.8) and a tube lens (f = 175 mm).
steps.searchable_summary: Direct scattered light from sample through imaging system to camera

steps.description: On the camera plane, the transmitted light interferes with a plane reference beam, which is obliquely incident on the camera.
steps.searchable_summary: Interfere transmitted light with reference beam on camera sensor

steps.description: The phase and amplitude of the scattered light can be retrieved from the recorded interference pattern, and then processed for reconstruction of the 3D RI map (see Fig. 1c).
steps.searchable_summary: Record the interference pattern using the camera

steps.description: The phase and amplitude of the scattered light can be retrieved from the recorded interference pattern, and then processed for reconstruction of the 3D RI map (see Fig. 1c).
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve phase and amplitude information from recorded interference patterns
outputs: Phase and amplitude data of scattered light

steps.description: Reconstruction of the RI maps was conducted using the principle previously proposed (Wolf, 1969). Detailed descriptions of the reconstruction algorithm can be found elsewhere (Kim, K et al., 2014;Lim et al., 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: Reconstruct 3D refractive index map from phase and amplitude data using ODT algorithms
inputs: Phase and amplitude data of scattered light
ODT reconstruction algorithm (based on Wolf 1969, Kim et al. 2014, Lim et al. 2015)

steps.description: Using ODT, 3D RI maps of a total of 92 entire pollen grains and 175 hollow shells of pollen grains were reconstructed. From Korean red pine trees (Pinus densiflora), a total of 6 pollen grains and 113 shells were imaged. From golden Korean red pine trees (Pinus densiflora Aurea), 17 pollen grains and 37 shells were imaged. From Japanese red pine trees (Pinus densiflora for. multicaulis), 38 pollen grains and 12 shells were imaged. From Japanese black pine trees (Pinus thunbergii), 31 pollen grains and 13 shells were imaged.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat ODT acquisition and reconstruction process for a specified number of pollen grains and shells from different pine species
inputs: Prepared sample slides for multiple pollen grains/shells (from Pinus densiflora, Pinus densiflora Aurea, Pinus densiflora for. multicaulis, Pinus thunbergii)
outputs: Collection of 3D refractive index maps for multiple samples (92 entire grains, 175 shells)
